evidence: ZFC/Class.lean:372-373 docstring literally: "The **Burali-Forti paradox**: ordinals form a proper class." Decl currently tagged Q1166618/Q903783/Q912188 (mathematical logic + generic) but missing the dedicated paradox QID.
verify_note: Class.lean:373 in ns ZFSet, docstring '**Burali-Forti paradox**'; Q1010269 label+enwiki match; tightest QID.

evidence: ZFC/Class.lean:62 `def univ : Class := ...` (the class of all ZFC sets). Q2649292 desc 'the class of all sets, necessarily a proper class' is exactly this. Decl currently untagged.
verify_note: Class.lean:62 def univ in ns Class; Q2649292 'class of all sets, necessarily proper' exact; no tighter item.

evidence: ZFC/Class.lean:288-289 docstring: "The definite description operator, which is `{x}` if `{y | A y} = {x}` and `∅` otherwise." Formalizes Russell-style iota/definite-description in ZFC classes. Decl currently untagged.
verify_note: iota docstring = 'definite description operator'; no iota/description-operator QID exists; Q265469 tightest.

evidence: ZFC/Class.lean:335 'A choice function on the class of nonempty ZFC sets.' Basic.lean module doc calls this the Axiom of choice. Currently only tagged Q4049983 (generic 'set theory'), missing dedicated AC QID.
verify_note: Basic.lean:24 module doc literally 'ZFSet.choice: Axiom of choice'; Q179692 exact per Mathlib's own doc.

evidence: ZFC/Ordinal.lean:42 `def IsTransitive (x : ZFSet) : Prop := ∀ y ∈ x, y ⊆ x`. Q671944 desc 'in set theory, a set whose elements are all subsets' is exactly this definition. Decl currently untagged.
verify_note: Ordinal.lean:42 def = forall y in x, y sub x; Q671944 desc matches verbatim; tightest.

evidence: ZFC/VonNeumann.lean:38 `def vonNeumann (o) : ZFSet`; docstring: 'defines the von Neumann hierarchy of sets V_o ... stratifies the universal class'. Decl currently untagged.
verify_note: VonNeumann.lean:38 in ns ZFSet; Q77887 alias 'von Neumann hierarchy' = V_o stages. NB: had generic Q4049983.

evidence: ZFC/VonNeumann.lean:38, same decl as V_o construction. Q18349448 'cumulative hierarchy' desc 'family of sets indexed by ordinal numbers' is the general concept the von Neumann universe instantiates; multi-QID (both apply).
verify_note: Tighter Q77887 wins (same decl, row 6); V_o is an INSTANCE of cumulative hierarchy - 'exact' overstates.

evidence: Veblen.lean:545 `abbrev epsilon := veblen 1`; docstring 'enumerates the fixed points of ω^⬝.' Q1403155 'ordinal that is the fixed point of the exponentiation map' matches exactly. Only generic Q191780/Q200787/Q3114739 tagged today.
verify_note: Veblen.lean:545 abbrev epsilon in ns Ordinal, enumerates fixed pts of w^.; Q1403155 enwiki 'Epsilon number' exact.

evidence: Veblen.lean:614 `def gamma := deriv (veblen · 0)`; docstring 'Γ₀ = gamma 0, the Feferman-Schutte ordinal.' Only Q3114739 ('large countable ordinal', generic parent) tagged today; gamma 0 is the specific named instance.
verify_note: Veblen.lean:614 docstring 'G0 = gamma 0, the Feferman-Schutte ordinal'; Q2264170 matches; special_case honest.

evidence: Ordinal/Veblen.lean:288 `def veblen : Ordinal.{u} → Ordinal.{u} → Ordinal.{u} := ...`; module docstring: 'We define the two-arguments Veblen function.' Q921930 desc 'hierarchy of normal functions' is exactly this. Decl currently untagged.
verify_note: Veblen.lean:288 def veblen in ns Ordinal; Q921930 'Veblen function' exact; catalog confirms untagged.

evidence: Ordinal/Veblen.lean:64-66 `def veblenWith (f) (o) : Ordinal → Ordinal := ...`, the o-th function in the Veblen hierarchy starting from arbitrary base f (veblen = veblenWith (ω ^ ·)). Generalizes the standard Veblen function. Decl currently untagged.
verify_note: Veblen.lean:65 veblenWith f, arbitrary base; veblen = veblenWith (w^.) so 'generalization' honest.

evidence: CantorNormalForm.lean:71 `def _root_.Ordinal.CNF (b o) : List (Ordinal × Ordinal)`; module titled 'Cantor Normal Form'. Mistagged with Q670235 (fundamental thm of arithmetic); Q108841139 is the correct QID (enwiki sitelink).
verify_note: def at CNF.lean:71; catalog confirms Q670235 (FTA) mistag; Q108841139 only CNF item on WD, enwiki sitelink.

evidence: Regular.lean:41 `structure IsRegular (c) where aleph0_le ...; le_cof_ord ...`. Q1193137 'cardinal that equals its own cofinality' is precisely this. Currently only Q1283623/Q191780 (generic) tagged, missing the dedicated QID.
verify_note: Regular.lean:41 fields aleph0_le + le_cof_ord = infinite cardinal equal to own cofinality; Q1193137 exact.

evidence: Regular.lean:374 `structure IsInaccessible (c) where aleph0_lt ...; isStrongPrelimit ...`; docstring 'uncountable regular strong limit cardinal.' Q1331373 matches exactly. Only generic Q1166618/Q12482/Q4049983 tagged today.
verify_note: Regular.lean:374 fields = uncountable+regular+strong limit = strongly inaccessible; Q1331373 exact.

evidence: Aleph.lean:702 `def beth (o) : Cardinal`; module doc: 'Cardinal.beth is the more standard function [skipping finite values].' Q831092 'transfinite numbers representing cardinality of continuous sets' matches exactly. Untagged.
verify_note: Aleph.lean:702, beth 0 = aleph0, succ = 2^; Q831092 exact. NB evidence 'untagged' false (had 3 generic QIDs).

evidence: Cardinal/Aleph.lean:572 `def preBeth (o : Ordinal.{u}) : Cardinal.{u} := ...`, the 'preliminary' variant of beth that additionally includes finite values. Same underlying named concept as Cardinal.beth. Decl currently untagged.
verify_note: Aleph.lean:572; beth docstring: 'For a version which starts at zero, see preBeth'; generalization honest.

evidence: Cofinality/Club.lean:37 `structure IsClub {α} (s : Set α) where dirSupClosed ...; isCofinal ...`; docstring 'called a **club set** when it is closed ... and cofinal.' Q310647 'closed cofinal subset of an ordinal' matches exactly. Untagged.
verify_note: Club.lean:37 top-level decl, docstring '**club set**'; Q310647 exact; LinearOrder form is the canonical home.

evidence: Cofinality/Club.lean:170 `def IsStationary (s) := ∀ t, IsClub t → (s ∩ t).Nonempty`; docstring 'a set is called stationary when it intersects all club sets.' Q1528401 matches exactly. Untagged.
verify_note: Club.lean:170 top-level def, 'intersects all club sets'; Q1528401 desc matches verbatim; exact.

evidence: FixedPointApproximants.lean:75 `def lfpApprox (a) : α`; module doc: 'ordinal-indexed approximation of fixed points of a monotone function in a complete lattice' (cites Cousot1979/Echenique2005, both build on Knaster-Tarski). Untagged.
verify_note: Module cites Cousot1979 'Constructive Versions of Tarski's FP Theorems'; no Cousot-Cousot QID; gen. is fair.

evidence: FixedPointApproximants.lean:206 `def gfpApprox (a) : α`, greatest-fixed-point counterpart to lfpApprox; same module frames both as the ordinal-indexed Knaster-Tarski-style approximation scheme on a complete lattice. Untagged.
verify_note: gfpApprox at :206, dual of lfpApprox, same Cousot/Tarski framing; Q609612 nearest item; generalization fair.

evidence: Rank.lean:69 `def rank (a) : Ordinal := (hwf.apply r a).rank` under `namespace IsWellFounded`; section header '### Rank in a well-founded relation'. Q338021 is the base relation concept. Untagged.
verify_note: Decl is the RANK function of a wf relation, not the wf-relation concept; 'exact' dishonest; no rank QID found.

evidence: Arithmetic.lean:158 `def limitRecOn {motive} (o) ...`; module doc 'limit recursion on ordinals, i.e. well-founded recursion separating out zero, successor, and limit cases' -- the ordinal special case of transfinite recursion. Untagged.
verify_note: Arithmetic.lean:158 in ns Ordinal; Q13368660 real item (en/de/es labels, cswiki); tighter than existing Q840810.

evidence: Lists.lean:67 `def Lists (α) := ...`; doc 'computable model of ZFA without infinity ... finite hereditary lists.' Lists Empty specializes to hereditarily finite sets; Lists α adds atoms. Untagged.
verify_note: HF sets = Finsets (Lists.lean:408, extensional quotient of Lists); Lists are lists, not sets. Re-propose on Finsets.
